creator: no@spam.thx
text:
>Submitter-Id:	current-users
>Originator:	alex
>Organization:	
>Confidential:	no
>Synopsis:	Re: kern/125637: src/sys/kern/uipc_sockbuf.c,v 1.171.2.3 causing kernel panic
>Severity:	non-critical
>Priority:	low
>Category:	kern
>Class:		sw-bug
>Release:	FreeBSD 7.0-STABLE i386
>Environment:	FreeBSD moshnroll 7.0-STABLE FreeBSD 7.0-STABLE #0: Mon Jun 30 12:40:54 CEST 2008 root@moshnroll:/usr/obj/usr/src/sys/ARUNDEL i386
>Description:
There's a reason I didn't submit my email address. I used to submit quite a lot of PRs. Unfortunately there is no way to tell the PR-system not to include my PR into the bugs mailinglist which is a very easy target for spammers. Also the actual PR-database can be queried very easily in order to harvest email adresses. After aprox. 2 years and about one duzen issued PR, my mail account got bombed with more than 600 new mails PER DAY!

I don't want that to happen again. That's why I didn't give out my actual mail adress. It would be a great if there'd be a way to

1. Tell the PR-system not to include a PR intp any of the the public mailinglists
and
2. somehow make the email address unreadable by spam-bots.

Cheers.
>How-To-Repeat:

>Fix:
